tasks.subject: [switch-agent-via-identity-chip] [P1] Add globalPaletteInitialSearch state and actions to app-store
tasks.description:
Add state and actions to the app store to support opening the command palette with a pre-populated search string.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/shared/model/app-store.ts`

**Step 1: Add to `AppState` interface** (after the `toggleGlobalPalette` line, around line 68):

```typescript
globalPaletteInitialSearch: string | null;
openGlobalPaletteWithSearch: (text: string) => void;
clearGlobalPaletteInitialSearch: () => void;
```

**Step 2: Add to store implementation** (after `toggleGlobalPalette` implementation, around line 217):

```typescript
globalPaletteInitialSearch: null,
openGlobalPaletteWithSearch: (text) =>
  set({ globalPaletteOpen: true, globalPaletteInitialSearch: text }),
clearGlobalPaletteInitialSearch: () =>
  set({ globalPaletteInitialSearch: null }),
```

**Technical details:**
- `globalPaletteInitialSearch` is transient state (not persisted to localStorage) — it is consumed once when the palette opens and then cleared
- `openGlobalPaletteWithSearch` atomically sets both `globalPaletteOpen: true` and the initial search text in a single `set()` call to avoid intermediate renders
- `clearGlobalPaletteInitialSearch` resets the value to `null` after consumption

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `globalPaletteInitialSearch` defaults to `null`
- Calling `openGlobalPaletteWithSearch('@')` sets `globalPaletteOpen` to `true` AND `globalPaletteInitialSearch` to `'@'` in a single state update
- Calling `clearGlobalPaletteInitialSearch()` resets `globalPaletteInitialSearch` to `null`
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- Existing tests pass without modification
tasks.activeForm: Adding initial search state to app store

tasks.subject: [switch-agent-via-identity-chip] [P1] Rewire AgentIdentityChip click to open palette with @ prefix
tasks.description:
Update the AgentIdentityChip component so that clicking it opens the command palette pre-filtered to agents (via `@` prefix) instead of opening the agent dialog.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/top-nav/ui/AgentIdentityChip.tsx`

**Step 1: Change the store selector** (line 25):

Replace:
```typescript
const setAgentDialogOpen = useAppStore((s) => s.setAgentDialogOpen);
```
With:
```typescript
const openGlobalPaletteWithSearch = useAppStore((s) => s.openGlobalPaletteWithSearch);
```

**Step 2: Update the click handler** (line 31):

Replace:
```typescript
onClick={() => setAgentDialogOpen(true)}
```
With:
```typescript
onClick={() => openGlobalPaletteWithSearch('@')}
```

**Step 3: Update the aria-label** (lines 34-38):

Replace:
```typescript
aria-label={
  agent
    ? `${agent.name} \u2014 agent settings`
    : 'Configure agent'
}
```
With:
```typescript
aria-label={
  agent
    ? `${agent.name} \u2014 switch agent`
    : 'Switch agent'
}
```

**Step 4: Update the tooltip text** (line 84):

Replace:
```tsx
<TooltipContent side="bottom">Agent settings</TooltipContent>
```
With:
```tsx
<TooltipContent side="bottom">Switch agent</TooltipContent>
```

**Acceptance criteria:**
- Clicking the chip calls `openGlobalPaletteWithSearch('@')` (not `setAgentDialogOpen(true)`)
- Tooltip reads "Switch agent" (not "Agent settings")
- When agent exists, aria-label reads `"<agentName> \u2014 switch agent"`
- When no agent, aria-label reads `"Switch agent"` (not "Configure agent")
- All other visual behavior unchanged (color dot, streaming pulse, name animation, chevron)
- TypeScript compiles without errors

[switch-agent-via-identity-chip] [P2] Remove AgentHeader from SessionSidebar and delete AgentHeader component
tasks.description:
Remove the AgentHeader component from the sidebar since agent identity now lives in the top navigation bar. The AgentHeader displayed directory context and agent switching, which are being relocated.

**File 1: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SessionSidebar.tsx`**

**Step 1: Remove the AgentHeader import** (line 21):
Delete this line:
```typescript
import { AgentHeader } from './AgentHeader';
```

**Step 2: Remove AgentHeader from the render** (lines 113-119):
In the `SidebarHeader`, remove the entire block:
```tsx
{selectedCwd && (
  <AgentHeader
    cwd={selectedCwd}
    onOpenPicker={() => setPickerOpen(true)}
    onOpenAgentDialog={() => setAgentDialogOpen(true)}
  />
)}
```

**Step 3: Clean up unused destructured store values.**
In the destructured `useAppStore()` call on line 30-31, check if `setPickerOpen` and `setAgentDialogOpen` are still used elsewhere in the component. `setPickerOpen` is no longer used (it was only passed to AgentHeader). `setAgentDialogOpen` is also only used for AgentHeader. Remove both from the destructured call if unused:

Change:
```typescript
const { setSidebarOpen, setPulseOpen, setPickerOpen, setAgentDialogOpen, setOnboardingStep } =
  useAppStore();
```
To:
```typescript
const { setSidebarOpen, setPulseOpen, setOnboardingStep } =
  useAppStore();
```

Verify `setPulseOpen` is still needed (it is used in the toast action on line 94).

**File 2: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/AgentHeader.tsx`**

Delete this file entirely.

**File 3: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/__tests__/AgentHeader.test.tsx`**

Delete this test file entirely.

**File 4: `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/index.ts`**

No changes needed — `AgentHeader` was never exported from this barrel file (confirmed: only `SessionSidebar`, `AgentContextChips`, and `SidebarFooterBar` are exported).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- `AgentHeader.tsx` file is deleted
- `AgentHeader.test.tsx` file is deleted
- `SessionSidebar.tsx` no longer imports or renders `AgentHeader`
- Unused store selectors (`setPickerOpen`, `setAgentDialogOpen`) removed from the SessionSidebar destructuring
- `SessionSidebar.test.tsx` assertion for `AgentHeader` rendering (`screen.getByLabelText('Change working directory')` on line 242) should be removed or updated — this was testing AgentHeader's directory button which no longer exists in the sidebar
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- All remaining tests pass

tasks.subject: [switch-agent-via-identity-chip] [P2] Add Edit Agent (Pencil) icon to SidebarFooterBar
tasks.description:
Add a Pencil icon button to the sidebar footer bar that opens the AgentDialog. This provides agent configuration access now that the AgentHeader is removed.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/ui/SidebarFooterBar.tsx`

**Step 1: Add Pencil import** (line 2):

Change:
```typescript
import { Sun, Moon, Monitor, Settings, Bug } from 'lucide-react';
```
To:
```typescript
import { Sun, Moon, Monitor, Settings, Bug, Pencil } from 'lucide-react';
```

**Step 2: Add `setAgentDialogOpen` to the store destructuring** (line 20):

Change:
```typescript
const { setSettingsOpen, devtoolsOpen, toggleDevtools } = useAppStore();
```
To:
```typescript
const { setSettingsOpen, setAgentDialogOpen, devtoolsOpen, toggleDevtools } = useAppStore();
```

**Step 3: Add the Pencil button before the Settings button** (insert before line 41, inside the `<div className="ml-auto flex items-center gap-0.5">`):

Add this JSX before the Settings button:
```tsx
<button
  onClick={() => setAgentDialogOpen(true)}
  className="text-muted-foreground/50 hover:text-muted-foreground rounded-md p-1 transition-colors duration-150"
  aria-label="Agent settings"
>
  <Pencil className="size-(--size-icon-sm)" />
</button>
```

The final order of buttons in the footer bar should be: Edit Agent (Pencil) | Settings (gear) | Theme toggle | Devtools (DEV only).

**Acceptance criteria:**
- A Pencil icon button appears in the sidebar footer bar, before the Settings gear button
- Clicking the Pencil button calls `setAgentDialogOpen(true)`
- The button has `aria-label="Agent settings"`
- The button uses the same styling as the existing Settings button: `text-muted-foreground/50 hover:text-muted-foreground rounded-md p-1 transition-colors duration-150`
- The icon uses `size-(--size-icon-sm)` consistent with other footer icons
- TypeScript compiles without errors
tasks.activeForm: Adding Edit Agent icon to sidebar footer

tasks.subject: [switch-agent-via-identity-chip] [P3] Add CWD display to AgentDialog

Add a working directory path display to the AgentDialog header, showing the project path via PathBreadcrumb with a FolderOpen icon. Verify it's in the existing import from `@/layers/shared/ui` — if not, add it. `FolderOpen` is not currently imported in this file. **Step 2: Add CWD display in the agent-exists state** (after the `ResponsiveDialogDescription` on line 74-76, inside the `ResponsiveDialogHeader`): After this existing block: ```tsx Agent configuration ``` Add: ```tsx
``` **Step 3: Add CWD display in the no-agent state** (after the "No agent registered" text on line 56, inside the `
`): Replace the no-agent inner content: ```tsx

No agent registered

``` With: ```tsx

No agent registered

``` **Acceptance criteria:** - When an agent exists, the CWD path (via PathBreadcrumb) appears below the "Agent configuration" description text in the dialog header, with a FolderOpen icon - When no agent is registered, the CWD path appears below the "No agent registered" text, also with a FolderOpen icon - `PathBreadcrumb` is called with `path={projectPath}`, `maxSegments={3}`, and `size="sm"` - The FolderOpen icon uses `size-3 shrink-0` classes - The CWD display uses `text-muted-foreground text-xs` styling - TypeScript compiles without errors

[switch-agent-via-identity-chip] [P4] Update SessionSidebar tests to remove AgentHeader assertions
tasks.description:
Update the SessionSidebar test suite to remove assertions that depended on the now-deleted AgentHeader component.

**File:** `apps/client/src/layers/features/session-list/__tests__/SessionSidebar.test.tsx`

**Step 1: Remove or update the "renders AgentHeader when selectedCwd is set" test** (lines 239-243).

This test currently asserts:
```typescript
it('renders AgentHeader when selectedCwd is set', () => {
  renderWithQuery(<SessionSidebar />);
  expect(screen.getByLabelText('Change working directory')).toBeDefined();
});
```

Delete this entire test case since AgentHeader is no longer rendered in the sidebar.

**Step 2: Remove the AgentHeader mock from the agent entity hooks mock if it was providing data specifically for AgentHeader.**

The existing mock on lines 67-78 mocks `useCurrentAgent` returning `{ data: null, isLoading: false }` and `useCreateAgent`. These are still needed for `AgentContextChips`, so leave the mock in place.

**Step 3: Verify the mock store still works.** The mock store on lines 31-53 includes `setPickerOpen` and `setAgentDialogOpen`. These can remain in the mock even though the component no longer uses them (the mock returns all state properties and unused ones are harmless). However, if you want to clean them up for accuracy, you can remove them.

**Acceptance criteria:**
- The test case "renders AgentHeader when selectedCwd is set" is removed
- All remaining tests pass without modification
- No test references `AgentHeader` or its specific UI elements (like 'Change working directory' aria-label)
- TypeScript compiles without errors
